instruction:
## s3 · Company Career Page Scrape (Scrapling-Powered)

Workspace    : `/home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room`
Scrapling    : `/home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room/Scrapling`
Board scraper: `/home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room/rabiuk-job-scraper`

### Goal
Use **Scrapling** to directly scrape company career pages for hardware / ML / embedded roles.
Scrapling's adaptive mode survives DOM changes, and `StealthyFetcher` bypasses anti-bot
protections (Cloudflare, Akamai, etc.) without manual Selenium/Playwright setup.

Target companies:
Google, Amazon, Apple, NVIDIA, Tesla, Meta, Microsoft, Waymo, Cruise, Anthropic,
OpenAI, Qualcomm, Texas Instruments, Broadcom, Marvell, DJI, ByteDance, TikTok,
Pony.ai, Plus.ai, NIO, Xpeng, ARM, Groq, Cerebras, SambaNova, d-Matrix.

---

### Step 0 — Ensure Scrapling is ready
```bash
cd /home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room
source .venv/bin/activate
python -c "import scrapling; print(scrapling.__version__)"  # verify installed
# If not installed:
pip install -e Scrapling/ --quiet
python -m scrapling install   # installs Playwright browsers
```

### Step 1 — Build/Update the Scrapling Career Page Scraper

Create or update `src/scrapers/career_scrapling.py`:
```python
"""Scrapling-powered company career page scraper."""
import logging
import json
import pathlib
from datetime import date, datetime
from typing import Any

from scrapling.fetchers import StealthyFetcher, DynamicFetcher

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

KEYWORDS = [
    'hardware', 'embedded', 'firmware', 'fpga', 'asic', 'vlsi', 'analog',
    'machine learning', 'ml engineer', 'computer vision', 'edge ai', 'tinyml',
    'neural', 'cuda', 'signal processing', 'robotics', 'intern', 'new grad',
]

# company -> (careers_url, CSS selector for job listing items)
COMPANY_TARGETS: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
    'NVIDIA':    ('https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/about-nvidia/careers/job-listing/?jobCategory=Engineering', '.job-card'),
    'Anthropic': ('https://www.anthropic.com/careers#open-roles', '.open-role'),
    'OpenAI':    ('https://openai.com/careers/search', '.careers-listing__item'),
    'Waymo':     ('https://waymo.com/joinus/', '.job-list-item'),
    'Qualcomm':  ('https://careers.qualcomm.com/careers/search?type=Intern', '.job-listing-item'),
    'Groq':      ('https://groq.com/careers/', '.job-listing'),
    'Cerebras':  ('https://www.cerebras.net/careers/', '.job-card'),
    'ARM':       ('https://careers.arm.com/search', '.result-list__item'),
}


def is_relevant(title: str, description: str = '') -> bool:
    """Return True if title or description matches a target keyword."""
    text = (title + ' ' + description).lower()
    return any(kw in text for kw in KEYWORDS)


def scrape_company(name: str, url: str, selector: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Scrape one company careers page with Scrapling."""
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    try:
        page = StealthyFetcher.fetch(url, headless=True, network_idle=True, timeout=30000)
    except Exception:
        logger.warning('StealthyFetcher failed for %s, trying DynamicFetcher', name)
        try:
            page = DynamicFetcher.fetch(url, headless=True, network_idle=True, timeout=30000)
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.error('Both fetchers failed for %s: %s', name, exc)
            return []

    # auto_save=True stores CSS fingerprints for adaptive recovery on future runs
    cards = page.css(selector, auto_save=True)
    for card in cards:
        title = (
            card.css('h2::text', auto_save=True).get('') or
            card.css('h3::text', auto_save=True).get('') or
            card.css('.title::text', auto_save=True).get('')
        ).strip()
        link = (card.css('a::attr(href)', auto_save=True).get('') or '').strip()
        loc  = (card.css('.location::text', auto_save=True).get('') or '').strip()
        if title and is_relevant(title):
            full_url = link if link.startswith('http') else f'{url.rstrip("/")}/{link.lstrip("/")}'
            results.append({
                'title':    title,
                'company':  name,
                'url':      full_url,
                'location': loc,
                'source':   'career_scrapling',
                'added_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            })
    logger.info('%s: %d relevant roles found', name, len(results))
    return results


def run_all() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Scrape all configured company career pages."""
    all_jobs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for name, (url, selector) in COMPANY_TARGETS.items():
        all_jobs.extend(scrape_company(name, url, selector))
    return all_jobs


if __name__ == '__main__':
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    jobs = run_all()
    out = pathlib.Path('/home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room') / f'career_scrapling_{date.today().isoformat()}.json'
    out.write_text(json.dumps(jobs, indent=2))
    print(f'Saved {len(jobs)} roles -> {out.name}')
```

### Step 2 — Run the Scrapling Career Scraper
```bash
cd /home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room
source .venv/bin/activate
python src/scrapers/career_scrapling.py
```
Note: total roles per company, any blocked/failed companies.

### Step 3 — Adaptive Recovery (if selectors break due to DOM change)
```python
from scrapling.fetchers import StealthyFetcher
# adaptive=True uses saved fingerprints to relocate elements even if CSS changed
page = StealthyFetcher.fetch(url, headless=True, network_idle=True)
cards = page.css(selector, adaptive=True)
```
If still 0 results: open the page in browser, find updated selector, update `COMPANY_TARGETS`.

### Step 4 — rabiuk Company Scraper (supplement)
```bash
cd /home/xwu/agent_playground/Job-war-room/rabiuk-job-scraper
python company_scraper/company_script.py --once 2>&1 | tail -20
```
Merge new results into `manually_added_jobs.json` if not duplicates.

### Step 5 — Log Results
Append to `ops_log.md`:
```markdown
### Company Career Scrape (Scrapling)
- Companies attempted (Scrapling) : <N>
- Relevant roles found             : <N>  -> career_scrapling_<date>.json
- rabiuk company scraper roles     : <N>
- Fetcher used                     : StealthyFetcher / DynamicFetcher / adaptive
- Failed companies                 : <list or 'none'>
- Errors                           : <list or 'none'>
```
